name: web-design-guidelines
description: Review UI code for Web Interface Guidelines compliance. Use when asked to "review my UI", "check accessibility", "audit design", "review UX", or "check my site against best practices".
capabilities: Fetch latest web interface guidelines from a remote source
Review specified files or file patterns against the guidelines
Identify deviations from established web design best practices
Output findings in a concise file:line format
author: antfu
securitySummary: Static analyzer flagged markdown syntax as security patterns. All findings are false positives. The backticks (lines 19, 25) are markdown code formatting, not shell execution. The YAML frontmatter (---) was misinterpreted as cryptographic syntax. The hardcoded URL is a documented feature for fetching guidelines from GitHub. This is a read-only review skill with no command execution capability.
valueStatement: Developers often build interfaces without following established best practices, leading to accessibility issues and poor user experience. This skill fetches the latest web interface guidelines from Vercel and automatically reviews code against them, providing actionable feedback in a standard file:line format.
userTitle: Review UI against web design guidelines
limitations: Cannot modify code to fix issues - only identifies problems
Guidelines are fetched at runtime and may change
Cannot access files outside the specified pattern or user input
Relies on externally hosted guidelines content

useCases.title: Frontend Code Review
useCases.description: A frontend developer wants to audit their React or Vue components for accessibility and design compliance before shipping. They invoke the skill with their component files to get a checklist of issues to fix.

useCases.title: Design System Audit
useCases.description: A design systems engineer needs to ensure their component library follows established web interface patterns. They run the skill across the entire library to identify inconsistencies.

useCases.title: Learning Best Practices
useCases.description: A new developer wants to learn what makes good web interfaces. They use the skill on their own code to see specific examples of where their design choices deviate from guidelines.

promptTemplates.title: Basic Review
promptTemplates.prompt: Use the web-design-guidelines skill to review the following files for web interface compliance: {file-pattern}
promptTemplates.scenario: When user wants to review specific files

promptTemplates.title: Full Project Audit
promptTemplates.prompt: Review all files matching {pattern} using web-design-guidelines for accessibility and design best practices.
promptTemplates.scenario: When user wants to audit a whole directory

promptTemplates.title: Interactive File Selection
promptTemplates.prompt: I want to review my UI code for web design compliance. Please use web-design-guidelines to check my files.
promptTemplates.scenario: When user wants guidance on which files to review

promptTemplates.title: Specific Component Check
promptTemplates.prompt: Check my {component-name} component against the web interface guidelines. Use web-design-guidelines to identify any issues.
promptTemplates.scenario: When user wants to review a specific component

bestPractices: Always review UI code against established guidelines before committing
Use the skill during code review to catch accessibility issues early
Combine with manual testing to verify findings in actual browsers
antiPatterns: Don't ignore findings without understanding why they are flagged
Don't assume the skill can automatically fix issues for you
Don't skip accessibility testing in favor of relying solely on this skill

faq.question: How often are the guidelines updated?
faq.answer: The skill fetches the latest guidelines from GitHub each time it runs, ensuring you always use the most current version.

faq.question: Can this skill fix the issues it finds?
faq.answer: No, the skill only identifies issues. You will need to manually apply the fixes based on the reported line numbers.

faq.question: What file types can it review?
faq.answer: Any text-based file containing UI code. The skill checks the content against the guidelines regardless of file extension.

faq.question: Does it work with any JavaScript framework?
faq.answer: Yes, the skill is framework-agnostic. It reviews the code content against general web interface principles.

faq.question: How reliable are the findings?
faq.answer: The skill applies automated rules from established guidelines. It catches common issues but should be supplemented with manual accessibility testing.

faq.question: Can I use this offline?
faq.answer: No, the skill requires network access to fetch the latest guidelines from GitHub before performing reviews.
